skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Creates AI-generated images through the hosted Image Skill CLI and API workflow, including guide mode, model selection, hosted media URLs, recoverable jobs, and cost controls.
skill.summary: Generate hosted AI images from prompts with guide mode, recoverable jobs, and cost controls.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography are mostly false positives from Markdown backticks and front matter text. Real risk remains medium because the skill instructs users to run remote npm CLI commands, use hosted network services, and write agent state under hidden home directories.

medium_findings.title: Remote CLI Execution Through npx
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to run `npx -y image-skill@latest` commands. This is legitimate for a hosted CLI workflow, but it executes code fetched from npm and should be treated as a supply-chain and command-execution risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and runnable. The context shows intended CLI use, so the risk is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Hosted Service and Payment Workflow Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill routes image generation through hosted Image Skill endpoints and references credits, wallet, payment loop, media spend, and durable hosted media URLs. This is core functionality, but users should understand the network and spending implications.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names hosted URLs and spending controls. The behavior appears disclosed and aligned with the skill purpose, so it is medium risk.

low_findings.title: Hidden Agent State Directory Writes
low_findings.description: The skill documents writing adapter state under `$HOME/.agents` and sets `CODEX_HOME` under `$HOME/.codex`. This is not secret harvesting, but hidden directory writes should be visible to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden paths are directly documented and used for local agent state. No evidence shows broad filesystem access or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and many Ruby backtick execution findings are false positives. The cited lines contain Markdown, YAML descriptions, or inline command names, not cryptographic code or Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file contains only Markdown and front matter. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithms or Ruby code execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned Latest npm Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples use `image-skill@latest` with `npx -y`, which fetches and runs the latest package without a pinned version.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The use of `@latest` is explicit in multiple commands. It is common for CLI installation, but it increases supply-chain review requirements.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Calls to Hosted Runtime
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on hosted Image Skill URLs and an external API endpoint for image generation and documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoints are clearly disclosed. No evidence found that the skill sends local secrets or hidden data to those endpoints.

content.value_statement: Agents often need generated images without managing provider accounts or local model servers. This skill provides a hosted CLI workflow with guide mode, model options, cost controls, and recoverable image jobs.
actual_capabilities: Runs a guide command before paid image creation.
Creates images from text prompts through the Image Skill CLI.
Supports model selection with examples for OpenAI image models.
Uses hosted media URLs, recoverable jobs, and cost receipts.
Documents Codex skill installation commands for this intent skill.
Provides a feedback command path for missing capabilities.
limitations: Requires network access to the hosted Image Skill service.
Runs npm CLI packages, so package provenance should be reviewed.
Image creation can require media spend when allowed by the user.
Model availability, latency, and policy support depend on the hosted service.

use_cases.title: Concept Visuals for Product Teams
use_cases.description: Generate quick product or feature visuals from plain-language prompts before commissioning final assets.

use_cases.title: Illustrations for Documentation
use_cases.description: Create hosted diagrams, cover images, or visual examples that agents can recover and cite later.

use_cases.title: Creative Drafts for Design Reviews
use_cases.description: Explore multiple visual directions with cost limits before moving work into a design tool.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for one generated asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create one hosted image of [subject] in [style]. Start with guide mode and keep spend disabled until ready.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Product Concepts
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for several visual directions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three image concepts for [product]. Use different compositions, keep each prompt concise, and include cost estimates before creation.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Documentation Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for publication-ready supporting visuals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a documentation image for [topic]. Use a clear visual metaphor, avoid text-heavy output, and return the hosted media link with receipt details.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Controlled Image Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt with model, budget, and recovery requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Image Skill guide mode for [campaign]. Select the model, set a per-image budget, create only after approval, and preserve job recovery details.

output_examples.input: Create a compact field camera on a stainless workbench.
output_examples.output: A hosted image result with the selected model, media URL, job status, cost receipt, and recovery information.

output_examples.input: Explore three visual concepts for a mobile app launch banner.
output: Three prompt directions with distinct compositions.
A guide result showing readiness and any missing inputs.
Final hosted image links after approval to spend.

output_examples.input: Create documentation artwork for an API authentication guide.
output_examples.output: A clean generated illustration, hosted link, estimated cost, and notes for citing the generated asset later.

best_practices: Run guide mode first and review missing inputs before creating paid media.
Use explicit prompts with subject, style, composition, and constraints.
Set a per-image budget and preserve hosted job details for recovery.
anti_patterns: Do not paste provider API keys into prompts or commands.
Do not approve media spend before checking guide output.
Do not rely on generated images for legal, medical, or safety-critical proof.

faq.question: Does this skill require a provider API key?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed around a hosted Image Skill identity rather than user-supplied provider credentials.

faq.question: Can it generate images from text prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented commands create images from prompts through the hosted Image Skill CLI.

faq.question: Does it spend money automatically?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to use guide mode first and create media only when spend is allowed.

faq.question: Where are generated images stored?
faq.answer: The skill describes durable hosted media URLs and recoverable jobs in the Image Skill runtime.

faq.question: Is this safe for private data?
faq.answer: Treat prompts as data sent to a hosted service. Do not include secrets, private credentials, or sensitive personal data.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing this skill?
faq.answer: Review the npm package provenance, hosted endpoint policy, spend controls, and hidden directory behavior.
